items.title: Mercy Malaysia calls for stronger NGO governance, transparency reforms
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Mercy Malaysia has called for stronger governance, better regulations and greater transparency of Malaysian non-governmental organisations (NGO) amid growing scrutiny over accountability and fund management. Its president Datuk Dr Ahmad Faizal Mohd Perdaus said the humanitarian sector could not afford to lose public trust following recent allegations and investigations involving humanitarian and humanitarian-leaning organisations. Ahmad Faizal said one of the sector's biggest challenges was Malaysia's fragmented NGO regulatory framework.

items.title: Malaysia managing global supply crisis on four fronts, says Economy minister
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The government will address the ongoing global supply crisis through four key priorities, namely securing essential supplies, optimising existing stock, curbing price increases, and capitalising on new energy opportunities, Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir said. Akmal added that the four priorities would guide the government's response as global pressures mounted. Akmal said the first priority was securing supply, particularly of food, energy, medicine, and essential goods.

items.title: Colorado reports hantavirus death unrelated to cruise outbreak
items.summary: WASHINGTON: An adult in Colorado has died after a confirmed case of hantavirus that is not linked to a recent outbreak aboard a cruise ship in the Atlantic, Colorado Department of Public Health and Environment officials said in a statement. The strain of hantavirus that caused the Colorado death occurs regularly in Colorado at this time of year, the statement said, and officials are investigating the source of exposure. Hantavirus is primarily spread by rodents but can be transmitted between people in rare cases and after prolonged, close contact.

items.title: MMA calls for immediate liquid nicotine relisting under Poisons Act following court ruling
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Medical Association (MMA) has called for the immediate relisting of liquid nicotine under the Poisons Act 1952 following a recent landmark High Court ruling. The High Court recently ruled that the 2023 delisting of liquid nicotine from the Poisons List was unlawful . Its president, Datuk Dr Thirunavukarasu Rajoo, said the association had repeatedly warned against the move since March 2023.

items.title: 'Not betrayal': Johor BN defends decision to contest alone
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Barisan Nasional's (BN) decision to contest the next Johor state election on its own cannot be deemed a betrayal of Pakatan Harapan (PH), says Johor Umno liaison committee deputy chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Maslan. Both BN and PH contested all 56 seats. "In this state election, BN did not betray anyone.

items.title: Zambry: No way... they're the ones who should adapt to our education system
items.summary: KUALA BERANG: The government will not bow to pressure from any group seeking to change the core entry requirements for public universities for their own interests, said Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abd Kadir. They are the ones who must adapt to our education system," he said. Zambry said the national education system has a clear structure with distinct roles between the Education Ministry, which manages schools, and the Higher Education Ministry, which handles university admissions.

items.title: Economy minister: Govt maintains RM300 diesel aid, adds RM100 interim assistance as global fuel prices rise
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 19 — The government will maintain the RM300 monthly BUDI MADANI Diesel cash assistance while introducing an additional interim RM100 aid payment for eligible recipients amid rising global fuel prices and growing supply chain uncertainty. Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir said the measures were part of Putrajaya’s targeted intervention strategy to shield lower-income groups and vulnerable sectors from the effects of the ongoing global supply crisis. Announcing the decision after today’s National Economic Action Council (MTEN) meeting, Akmal Nasrullah said the government remained focused on ensuring the rakyat were not left to bear rising costs alone.

items.title: Southeast Asia's e-commerce market projected to reach US$289.8bil by 2029
items.summary: According to a leading regional payment platform in Southeast Asia, 2C2P by Antom, the region's e-commerce market is expected to grow by 85.4 per cent to reach US$289.8 billion by 2029. The report said growth in digital payments across Southeast Asia's e-commerce market between 2024 and 2029 is expected to encompass several payment methods, including a 104 per cent increase in domestic payments, which are projected to rise to US$92 billion in 2029 from US$45.1 billion in 2024. Additionally, the growth in digital payments will also be driven by mobile wallets, which are expected to reach US$79.0 billion in 2029 from US$38.2 billion in 2024, while Buy Now Pay Later (BNPL) transactions are projected to surge 174 per cent to US$18.9 billion from US$6.9 billion over the same period.

items.title: Woman stabbed in neck stable after 10-hour surgery
items.summary: The victim's sister said the operation began at 9am and concluded at 7pm yesterday, adding that the victim remains under treatment in the intensive care unit. She said the family is looking after the victim's two children while she recovers in the hospital. Kota Setar district police chief Assistant Commissioner Syed Basri Syed Ali said the victim is currently in stable condition.

items.title: ‘Come once a week’: Selangor Sultan asks Ronnie Liu, DAP rep to visit Rukun Negara monument over pig farming controversy
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 19 — Sultan of Selangor Sultan Sharafuddin Idris Shah has called on all Selangor assemblymen to visit Dataran Selangor twice a month to read and understand the Rukun Negara displayed on a newly built monument plaque. His Royal Highness said the plaque, placed in front of the Selangor state flagpole, was built and installed by the Selangor Public Works Department together with benches for visitors. Sultan Sharafuddin said he had fully funded the cost of the Rukun Negara monument plaque and the benches.

items.title: Velesto Energy's net profit falls to RM27.85mil in Q1
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Velesto Energy Bhd's net profit for the first quarter ended March 31, 2026 (Q1 2026) declined to RM27.85 million versus RM52.60 million a year ago. Revenue fell to RM183.35 million against RM224.64 million previously, dragged down by lower average daily charter rates during the quarter. Its drilling services segment's revenue fell to RM179.9 million from RM221.1 million in Q1 2025, mainly attributed to a lower average daily charter rate (Q1 2026: US$108,000 per day; Q1 2025: US$127,000 per day).

items.title: Zambry: SPM remains main entry requirement for IPTA admissions
items.summary: KUALA BERANG, May 19 — Admission to public higher education institutions (IPTA) through the Unit Pusat Universiti (UPU) Online is still based on the Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM), said Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Zambry Abd Kadir. He said the government, through a Cabinet decision, has set that the basic requirement for admission to higher education institutions via UPU Online is SPM, regardless of the stream or type of school attended. The requirements under UPU Online are based on SPM.

items.title: Inmate jailed 20 months for fracturing cellmate's eye socket over dirty toilet
items.summary: MUAR: A drug detainee was sentenced to 20 months' jail by the magistrate's court here today after pleading guilty to punching a fellow inmate and fracturing his eye socket during a fight at the Muar Correctional Centre two months ago. Police investigations showed that the fight broke out because the accused lost his temper after the victim neglected toilet cleanliness. Investigations revealed that the accused punched the victim once in the face, causing severe injuries.

items.title: Defence minister: Navy to decide replacement for cancelled Norwegian Naval Strike Missile system
items.summary: LUMUT, May 19 —The Ministry of Defence (Mindef) has left it to the Royal Malaysian Navy (RMN) to assess alternatives to replace the Naval Strike Missile (NSM) system, after its supply was cancelled by a Norwegian defence contractor. Mohamed Khaled said replacing the NSM system would involve additional costs, including training personnel and integrating a new system with existing platforms. “Then there are training costs and the need to ensure integration between the new system and the existing combat system.

items.title: Lorry driver pleads not guilty to assaulting stepdaughter
items.summary: BATU PAHAT: A lorry driver was charged at the Yong Peng magistrate's court here today with allegedly assaulting his 12-year-old stepdaughter. The accused pleaded not guilty after the charges were read before magistrate Arun Noval Dass. The case was framed under Section 323 of the Penal Code for causing hurt, read together with Section 326A for domestic abuse.

items.title: Petronas Gas's Q1 net profit slips 6.4pct to RM438.7mil
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Petronas Gas Bhd's net profit fell 6.4 per cent to RM438.7 million in the first quarter of financial year 2026 (Q1 FY26) from RM468.8 million a year ago. Petronas Gas' revenue slipped 0.6 per cent to RM1.58 billion from RM1.59 billion in Q1 2025. Petronas Gas said its outlook for 2026 is expected to remain resilient despite margin pressures, particularly in the utilities segment, amid higher fuel gas prices driven by ongoing global geopolitical uncertainties.

items.title: Malayan Flour to build Vietnam plant as it spends RM100mil on capacity expansion, automation
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malayan Flour Mills Bhd (MFM) has allocated RM100 million in capital expenditure (capex) over the next two financial years to increase its daily flour milling capacity in Vietnam and drive automation in Malaysia. MFM executive deputy chairman cum managing director Teh Wee Chye said the expansion will increase milling capacity in the country to 2,500 tonnes per day from 2,000 tonnes per day to meet rising regional demand, as existing operations are already running at full capacity On why a larger allocation was directed towards Vietnam, Teh said Vietnam's economy is expanding rapidly and continues to show a tremendous appetite for growth. Teh said.

items.title: RHB Research upgrades 99 Speed Mart to 'Buy'
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: RHB Research has upgraded its call for 99 Speed Mart Retail Holdings Bhd to "Buy" on the back of the company's improved financial performance for the first quarter (Q1). The firm said that 99 Speed Mart's results met expectations underpinned by an exciting sales momentum and disciplined cost control. "Valuation is looking more palatable now given 99 Speed Mart's resilient fundamentals to offer earnings visibility amidst the ongoing market challenges.

items.title: Ex-Perlis MB's son acquitted of RM19,505 false MACC claim charge
items.summary: KANGAR: The Sessions Court here today acquitted and discharged the son of former Perlis menteri besar Mohd Shukri Ramli from a charge of submitting a false claim of RM19,505.10. Judge Datuk Nu'aman Mahmud Zuhudi ruled that the prosecution had failed to establish a prima facie case against Mohd Syafeeq Mohd Shukri, 37, after examining witness testimony. Syafeeq was represented by lawyer Mohd Fadhly Yaacob.

items.title: Sabah probes suspected ivory poaching after mutilated Bornean elephant found in Tongod forest reserve
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, May 19 — Sabah authorities are investigating the death of a Bornean elephant found mutilated in a forest reserve in Tongod, believed to be linked to ivory poaching. State Tourism, Culture and Environment Minister Datuk Jafry Ariffin said he was deeply concerned by the discovery of the dead elephant within the nursery forest area of the Sungai Pinangah Forest Reserve. According to reports, the Kinabatangan Wildlife team received a complaint at approximately 8.25am on May 18, 2026 regarding a dead elephant suspected to be linked to wildlife crime.

items.title: Malaysia Marine's earnings rise 17pct on stronger heavy engineering contributions
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia Marine and Heavy Engineering Holdings Bhd (MMHE) posted a 17 per cent increase in net profit to RM14.3 million for the first quarter ended March 31, 2026 (Q1 FY26) from RM12 million a year ago, supported by stronger contributions from its heavy engineering segment. MMHE's revenue rose 15.2 per cent to RM521.9 million from RM453.1 million last year. In line with the higher revenue, the company recorded an operating profit of RM18.1 million, up 17.5 per cent from RM15.4 million previously.

items.title: Zara's mum did not want a post-mortem, court hears
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: Noraidah Lamat did not want a post-mortem to be conducted on the late Zara Qairina Mahathir, the Coroner's Court heard today. Nur Shira Abdullah said her sister-in-law had told her privately in a hospital ward when there were no visitors present. "Zara's mother had said that she did not want a post-mortem as she did not want her child to be 'hurt' or 'cut open'.

items.title: Amirudin calls for grassroots cooperation to curb cable theft disrupting digital services in Selangor
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, May 19 — Local communities have been urged to play a more active role in providing early information to help authorities combat cable theft and vandalism involving telecommunications infrastructure in Selangor. Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari said public cooperation is crucial to ensure immediate action can be taken to prevent acts of sabotage, which not only damage public facilities but also disrupt the public’s digital access. As I mentioned, digital infrastructure is important for sustaining daily life in the state of Selangor.

items.title: Dennis Lim named new Penang police chief in police reshuffle
items.summary: Federal police corporate communications chief Assistant Commissioner Julina Salehuddin said Lim will serve as Acting Commissioner of Police, effective June 20. The current Terengganu JKDNKA chief, Assistant Commissioner Azrul Hisham Mohd Shaffei, has been appointed Johor Baru Selatan district police chief. "Bandar Baru district police chief in Kedah, Superintendent Syamsul Sinring, has been appointed Kulim district police chief in Kedah," she said.

items.title: #NSTviral: Man apologises after viral video of cat, kittens sparks outrage
items.summary: KUANTAN: A trader has come under intense public scrutiny after a split-second decision captured him throwing a box containing a cat and kittens in a manner widely described as heartless. The posting and screengrabs, which went viral on Facebook, claimed that the 44-year-old's actions had resulted in the deaths of six kittens. However, Ho Kian Loon dismissed the allegations as untrue, saying he was now caring for the mother cat and all her kittens.

items.title: Kerjaya Prospek wins RM174.2mil IJM Land contract in Seremban
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Kerjaya Prospek Group Bhd's subsidiary Kerjaya Prospek (M) Sdn Bhd has secured a RM174.2 million contract from IJM Land Bhd's subsidiary RB Land Sdn Bhd for the construction of a high-rise residential development in Seremban, Negri Sembilan. In a statement, Kerjaya said the contract involves the main building works for a 44-storey serviced apartment block comprising 778 apartment units and 15 retail lots, among others. Kerjaya chief executive officer and executive director Tee Eng Tiong said the contract marked the group's second project secured in Seremban this year, reflecting growing development momentum in Negri Sembilan.

items.title: Navy to identify alternatives for NSM after Norway missile deal falls through
items.summary: LUMUT: The Defence Ministry has instructed the Royal Malaysian Navy (RMN) to identify alternatives to replace the Naval Strike Missile (NSM) system following procurement complications with Norway. Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said the matter is being closely reviewed after Norway's decision affected the delivery of the surface-to-surface missile system intended for the Littoral Combat Ship (LCS) programme. LCS 1 is at 84 per cent; LCS 2 at 79 per cent; LCS 3 at 71 per cent; LCS 4 at 60 per cent, and LCS 5 at 49 per cent.

items.title: Penang 75pc ready for snap polls, says Chow Kon Yeow
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, May 19 — Penang is about 75 per cent ready to face snap polls, said Penang Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow. He said there is no fixed point at which a state can be considered fully ready for elections, whether at 80 or 100 per cent. ““If you ask me, I always give the answer 75 per cent, not completely prepared, but educated prepared if there is a possibility,” he said when he was asked whether the state is ready to face snap polls.

items.title: Chow: Penang '75pct prepared' if snap polls called
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: Penang is "75 per cent prepared" to face a snap general election should Parliament be dissolved unexpectedly. Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow, however, stressed that the state government's focus remained on delivering its 2023 unity government manifesto rather than election preparations. Chow said Penang had achieved more than a 90 per cent implementation rate for manifesto pledges categorised as either completed or ongoing.

items.title: #NSTviral: Motorists stop female driver after wrong-way travel on Dash highway [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Cars whizzed past as a female driver drove against traffic on the Damansara-Shah Alam Elevated Expressway (Dash), a three-lane dual carriageway in each direction, before motorists intervened to stop the vehicle. The two-minute clip, shared by the DashCam Malaysia Facebook page, showed the woman driving on the wrong side of the highway as oncoming motorists flashed their headlights. The vehicle eventually came to a halt.

items.title: Zambry: BN will stay with unity govt until end of mandate
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 19 — Barisan Nasional (BN) will continue to preserve understanding in the Unity Government until the end of the administration’s mandate to ensure national stability and the well-being of the people, said its secretary-general Datuk Seri Zambry Abd Kadir. Touching on developments in Johor, Zambry said BN respects the stance voiced by the state’s BN regarding the direction and preparations for the upcoming state election. Last Saturday, Johor BN chairman Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi announced that the coalition would contest all 56 state assembly seats.

items.title: Read, understand Rukun Negara, Selangor ruler tells DAP rep, Ronnie Liu [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Sultan of Selangor Sultan Sharafuddin Idris Shah has told a DAP assemblyman and a former DAP leader to read the Rukun Negara every week to understand its contents and become courteous citizens who respect the royal institution. Sultan Sharafuddin said all assemblymen in the state should go to Dataran Selangor twice a month to read the Rukun Negara inscribed on a plaque at the square. "I encourage them to go to Dataran Selangor every week to understand and appreciate the Rukun Negara."

items.title: MBI Selangor denies involvement in Bernam Maritime Industrial Park launch, says use of its name and logo unauthorised
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, May 19 — Menteri Besar Selangor (Incorporated) (MBI Selangor) has denied any involvement in the organisation, management, ownership, promotion or operation of the Bernam Maritime Industrial Park project and its launch ceremony, as circulated by Bernam Shipyard Holdings Sdn Bhd. “MBI Selangor has conducted an initial check with the Sabak Bernam District Council (MDSB) and, based on the information currently available, there is no official confirmation linking MBI Selangor and MDSB to the project or the launch ceremony. “MBI Selangor views seriously the unauthorised use of its name, logo and branding elements in promotional and publicity materials related to the event and project.

items.title: 17-year-old school dropout pleads guilty to raping ex-girlfriend
items.summary: BATU PAHAT: A 17-year-old school dropout pleaded guilty at the Yong Peng magistrate's court here to raping a student who is also his former girlfriend. According to the charge sheet, he was accused of raping the 15-year-old victim at a house at 3pm in May last year. Police investigations revealed the incident occurred when the victim travelled to the accused's house using a p-hailing service, where he invited her inside to commit the act.

items.title: Affin Bank's momentum seen slowing if uncertainty persists
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Affin Bank Bhd's loan growth momentum remains solid for now but may moderate if geopolitical uncertainties persist beyond June, potentially delaying corporate pipelines and deal conversions. Hong Leong Investment Bank Bhd (HLIB) said despite near-term headwinds, Affin's net interest margin is expected to recover towards the 1.5 per cent target. "Subsequently, we introduce our FY28 forecasts, implying 10.7 per cent earnings growth," it said.

items.title: Sateerah Hassan quits as Putrajaya PKR Women deputy chief
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 19 — Sateerah Hassan has confirmed her resignation as deputy chief of the Women’s wing of Parti Keadilan Rakyat’s (PKR) Putrajaya division. Putrajaya PKR Women chief Fasyarini Azahari said the party acknowledged Sateerah’s decision and respected her choice, according to a report published in Sinar Harian today. She said the party appreciated Sateerah’s contributions, commitment and service throughout her time in the leadership line-up.

items.title: PM proud of Malaysia's economic growth despite Middle East conflict [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's economic growth has exceeded projections despite the severe oil price shock triggered by the Middle East crisis, says Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. "Alhamdulillah, despite all that, our economic growth performance last year and in the first quarter of this year has been higher than projected," Anwar said at the National Sports Awards 2025 here today. Anwar said the achievement was notable given that national economic projections were based on oil trading at around US$70 per barrel.

items.title: Malaysia seeks RM1b from Norwegian firm over cancelled missile export licence, says Khaled
items.summary: LUMUT, May 19 — The Ministry of Defence (Mindef) has issued a notice of demand to a Norwegian manufacturer following the cancellation of an export licence for the Naval Strike Missile (NSM) anti-ship and land-attack missile system. Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said the notice, seeking compensation for both direct and indirect losses, was sent to the company today. He said the Norwegian government had unilaterally revoked the export licence without prior discussions with Malaysia, despite the government having fulfilled all contractual obligations.

items.title: Kelantan Health Dept warns against frozen food products from Thailand
items.summary: KOTA BARU: The Kelantan Health Department has urged parents to monitor their children against purchasing frozen food products from Thailand, especially sausages, due to safety and compliance concerns. Its director Mohd Azman Yacob said enforcement operations conducted by health officers stationed at Immigration, Customs, Quarantine and Security (ICQS) complexes in the state found that many food items were brought into the country from Sungai Golok, mostly by traders. "Throughout last year, the most frequently seized food items at the Rantau Panjang ICQS were various types of frozen sausages.

items.title: Aunt breaks down when videos of Zara played in court
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: The Coroner's Court had to stand down proceedings after the aunt of the late Zara Qairina Mahathir broke down in tears when videos of family moments were played. Lawyer Shahlan Jufri, who is representing Zara Qairina's mother Noraidah Lamat, showed several videos featuring the teenage sharing her school experiences and spending time with her cousins, including recording themselves doing the "Velocity" TikTok trend during Hari Raya. Nur Shida also broke down when she testified about collecting Zara Qairina's belongings from the school.

items.title: Ex-Perlis MB’s son acquitted of false claims charge involving RM19,000
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 19 — The Sessions Court in Kangar, Perlis, today acquitted and discharged former Perlis Menteri Besar Mohd Shukri Ramli’s son, Mohd Syafeeq Mohd Shukri, 37, of a charge involving the submission of a false claim worth more than RM19,000, without calling for his defence. According to a report published by Berita Harian , Judge Datuk Nu’aman Mahmud Zuhudi made the ruling after finding that the prosecution had failed to establish a prima facie case against the accused at the close of the prosecution’s case. In delivering the judgment, Nu’aman said the court’s role at the end of the prosecution stage was to determine whether a prima facie case had been proven.

items.title: Indonesia signed letter but made no commitment to US on airspace access, says defence minister
items.summary: JAKARTA: Indonesia's defence minister said on Tuesday that he had signed a letter of intent last month with US Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth on granting US military aircraft access to Indonesian airspace, but said no commitment was made. We uphold the constitution and our national interests," Sjafrie said. The issue of overflight access created controversy in Indonesia last month.

items.title: ‘A leopard cannot change its spots’: Lim Guan Eng labels Khairy, Umno ‘fake progressives’ over defence of Dr Akmal’s politics
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 19 — DAP adviser Lim Guan Eng today accused Khairy Jamaluddin and Umno of being “fake progressives” after the former minister defended Umno Youth chief Datuk Dr Muhamad Akmal Saleh’s style of politics. In a statement, Lim said Khairy’s assertion that Akmal’s political approach remained necessary in Umno was disappointing, especially after previously positioning himself as a progressive and non-racial politician. Lim said Khairy appeared to suggest that Umno president Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi tolerated Akmal’s controversial “culture war politics” because of what he described as a “clear and present danger” from the political right dominated by Perikatan Nasional.

items.title: Former Sabah Water Dept director found guilty of money laundering, wife and deputy acquitted
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, May 19 — The Sessions Court here has found former Sabah Water Department director Ag Mohd Tahir Mohd Talib guilty of 12 charges of money laundering. Two others — Ag Mohd Tahir’s wife Fauziah Piut and former deputy director Lim Lam Beng @ Lim Chee Hong — were discharged and acquitted of all their charges. Fauziah had 19 charges while Lim faced four charges.

items.title: Police detain 15 over dangerous flare stunt ride in Bukit Bintang linked to Dengkil clothing shop
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 19 — Police have detained 15 people, including a woman, less than 24 hours after they allegedly ignited flares and rode dangerously around Bukit Bintang in an incident that went viral on social media. According to The Star, Dang Wangi police chief ACP Sazalee Adam said the suspects, aged between 17 and 30, were arrested in Batu Caves, Shah Alam, Dengkil and Kajang during an operation conducted between noon and 8pm yesterday. He reportedly said that police seized two electric scrambler motorcycles and a four-wheel-drive vehicle believed to have been used in the stunt.

items.title: Penang volunteer fire brigades lodge police report over Facebook claims linking them to money laundering
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, May 19 — Penang volunteer fire brigades have lodged a police report against a Facebook user over allegations linking the organisations to money laundering and tax evasion activities. He said the comment made by “Yoong Hai” also linked donations made to volunteer fire brigades to money laundering and tax evasion schemes. Meanwhile, H’ng said there are 38 volunteer fire brigade teams in Penang.

items.title: Over 20 villagers band together to capture 300kg crocodile in KK near Sabah Legislative House
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 19 — More than 20 villagers joined forces to capture a crocodile weighing about 300 kilogrammes in Kota Kinabalu earlier today. The crocodile had reportedly been seen surfacing near the Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) beach over the past few weeks, raising concern among residents and visitors. Meanwhile, Kota Kinabalu Civil Defence Force (APM) officer Capt (PA) Aizex Junior said the District Operations Control Centre received a call at 8.02am.

items.title: Penang gets tough on litterbugs: Body cams, 24-hour enforcement rolled out under new community service order scheme
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, May 19 — Officers equipped with body cameras will be deployed as part of Penang’s new Community Service Order (PKM) enforcement against litterbugs from July 1 onwards. State Local Government and Town and Country Planning Committee chairman H’ng Mooi Lye said enforcement action will also be carried out round-the-clock. He said the use of body cameras, along with photographic and video recordings, will serve as evidence during enforcement operations.

items.title: Malaysia beats growth forecasts despite oil price surge above US$100, says Anwar
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 19 — Malaysia’s economic performance has continued to outperform initial forecasts, despite global pressures and rising oil prices, according to Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. He said that economic projections had earlier been based on oil prices of around US$70 (RM278) per barrel, but these have since climbed to above US$100 amid shifting global conditions. “When earlier economic projections were made, oil prices were around US$70 per barrel.

items.title: No cuts to programmes: Govt keeps sports funding intact, athletes’ welfare top priority, says Anwar
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 19 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today said that there will be no financial cuts to sports programmes despite ongoing economic pressures. He said the government remains committed to safeguarding the country’s sporting development. “Despite financial constraints, I have assured the Cabinet that sports programmes and athlete welfare will be fully safeguarded.

items.title: Malaysia targets 902 clinics for integrated lung health rollout by end-2026, says Health Minister
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 19 — Malaysia has stepped up its efforts to translate the World Health Assembly’s Lung Health Resolution into practical action by pursuing an integrated healthcare approach and forging strong public-private partnerships to improve respiratory health outcomes nationwide. Health Minister Datuk Seri Dzulkefly Ahmad said that over the past year, Malaysia has focused on implementing the resolution through two key strategies: strengthening integrated lung health services at the primary care level and operationalising a whole-of-society approach under the Lung Health Initiative Malaysia. He said this in a speech for the Lung Health Forum: From Resolution to Action - Government Leadership on Lung Health, to mark the 79th World Health Assembly (WHA79), which was shared by the Ministry of Health (MOH) here today.

items.title: High Court orders Papagomo to pay RM280,000 to Lim Guan Eng, son in defamation case
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 19 — The High Court has ordered blogger Wan Azri Wan Deris, better known as Papagomo, to pay RM280,000 in damages after ruling that he defamed former DAP chairman Lim Guan Eng and his son in a social media post. The court ordered Wan Azri to pay RM200,000 to Guan Eng and RM80,000 to his son. He was also ordered to pay RM30,000 in costs to Guan Eng and RM10,000 to his son.

items.title: Villagers on alert as sun bear returns nightly, kills goats and damages bee hives in Terengganu
items.summary: JERTIH, May 19 — Residents of Kampung Alor Belanga near Kampung Alor Keladi here are concerned over the presence of a sun bear that has been roaming the village in search of food since April. “Previously, the bear only damaged stingless bee hives and coconut trees to feed on honey and coconuts, but lately it has allegedly attacked and eaten villagers’ livestock. “The latest incident occurred last Thursday when the bear attacked and ate a resident’s goat.

items.title: Aunt recounts bruises, severe head injury seen on Zara Qairina during treatment and post-mortem handling at inquest
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 19 — The Coroner’s Court was told today about visible injuries and bruising found on the body of Zara Qairina Mahathir during her treatment and later handling at Queen Elizabeth Hospital (HQE) on July 17 last year. Nur Shira, who is also the aunt of Zara and sister-in-law of the victim’s mother Noraidah Lamat, said she was shown the body at the hospital and later during arrangements, where she noticed several concerning physical conditions. The body, she said, appeared yellowish in colour.

items.title: Malaysia rolls out regional urban agenda platform at UN forum to boost coordinated city development
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 19 — Malaysia has launched the Asia Pacific Urban Agenda Platform (AP-UAP) at the opening of the Malaysia Pavilion during the 13th World Urban Forum (WUF13) in Baku, Azerbaijan, on Tuesday, marking a significant milestone in regional cooperation by transitioning from fragmented discussions towards a structured, action-oriented mechanism. Housing and Local Government Minister Nga Kor Ming, who is also the president of the United Nations Human Settlements Programme (UN-Habitat) Assembly, said the AP-UAP is vital for transforming urban efforts across the Asia-Pacific into a collective and structured ecosystem, enabling faster implementation from policy to action. Available in both digital and physical formats, the publication highlights Malaysia’s urban planning evolution, public housing policies, community-driven development approaches and efforts to build more inclusive, resilient, and people-centred cities.

items.title: Four Malaysians continue voyage towards Gaza despite flotilla interceptions
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 19 — Four Malaysians are still continuing their journey towards Gaza even as most vessels in the Global Sumud Flotilla (GSF 2.0) convoy have been intercepted and detained by Israeli forces. Of the 44 ships in the Global Sumud Flotilla (GSF 2.0), only eight remain active, including Nablus (Alcyone), Beit Hanoun (Zefiro) and Isdud (Don Juan), which carry the four Malaysians. Five other active GSF vessels are Hawsha (Cabo Blanco), Balad al-Sheik (Girolama), Kasrı Sadabat, Ramle (Sirius) and Akka (Andros).

items.title: Gig worker social security cover active after first task acceptance, says Socso
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 19 — Social security protection for gig workers under the Gig Workers Act 2025 (Act 872) will take effect once they have registered with a platform provider and accepted their first task. The Social Security Organisation (Socso), in a statement today, said the postpaid protection mechanism provides one-year coverage for gig workers upon fulfilling the two initial prerequisites. “Socso protection status remains active after the two initial prerequisites are fulfilled, even if gig workers do not receive bookings for several days or have not yet paid the minimum monthly contribution of RM13.10.

items.title: Four teens arrested for allegedly strangling, assaulting e-hailing driver over unpaid fare in Alor Gajah
items.summary: He said the suspects comprised two teenage boys aged 15 and 17, and two teenage girls aged 14. “A few minutes later, three of the suspects — the 17-year-old boy and the two girls — suddenly turned aggressive and attacked the victim,” he said today. “The suspects also threatened the victim not to seek help.

items.title: Woman, 84, fatally burned in Sandakan house fire, found in living room
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 19 — An 84-year-old woman was found charred after a two-storey house she was living in caught fire in Jalan Fook Hin, Batu 12, Sandakan, Sabah, last night. Sandakan Fire and Rescue Station chief Jimmy Lagung said the department received a report on the incident at 8.38pm before deploying 13 personnel and three fire engines to the scene. Firefighters used water from the fire engine tanks to extinguish the blaze.

items.title: Wangsa Maju MP says attendance at Rafizi, Nik Nazmi event was to honour invitation; respects their PKR exit decision
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 19 — Wangsa Maju Member of Parliament Zahir Hassan has clarified that his attendance at a political direction programme involving former economy minister Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli and former natural resources and environmental sustainability minister Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad was to fulfil an invitation from fellow comrades. In a Facebook post, Zahir said he was invited to attend the programme, which he described as an opportunity to witness their sharing on future political direction. He also said he respects their decision to vacate their parliamentary seats effective May 18, 2026, and to leave Parti Keadilan Rakyat (PKR) effective May 19, 2026.
